name: Levofloxacin
executiveSummary: Cervicitis, urethritis due to C. trachomatis and gonococcus. Severe infections by multidrug-resistant organisms. Mycobacterial infections.
indications:
## Main indications

Cervicitis, urethritis due to C. trachomatis and gonococcus. Severe infections by multidrug-resistant organisms. Mycobacterial infections.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

pediatrico.dose: Children: information is very limited, some centers recommend: 6 months to 5 years: 10 mg/kg/dose every 12 h, maximum dose: 500 mg/day; over 5 years: 10 mg/kg/dose every 24 h, maximum dose: 1 g/day. Adults: 500 mg every 24 h; severe infections: 750 mg every 24 h; tuberculosis treatment: 15 - 20 mg/kg/day every 24 h; travelers diarrhea: 500 mg every 24 h for 3 days.
pediatrico.presentation: Tablets: 500 mg; 20 mL vial: 25 mg/ml; Suspension (compounded preparation): 50 mg/ml
pediatrico.notes: Aluminum and magnesium-based antacids should be taken at least 2 h before or after levofloxacin. May prolong theophylline half-life. Additive effects with medications that prolong the QT interval. See tuberculosis treatment guide

stability:
## Stability

- IV diluted per prescribing information; protect from light.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Diarrhea, nausea, vaginitis, rash, insomnia, hepatic toxicity. Hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia. Tendon ruptures (most frequently Achilles tendon) from 48 h of treatment.
